name: Mixed Berry Protein Cheesecake
description: FIRST DRAFT (to tune): mixed-berry flavor of the vanilla protein cheesecake base - a sugar-free mixed-berry swirl (punched up with imitation blueberry extract) marbled through the batter and a berry scatter on top. ALSO the test bed for a water-bath bake at 300F to get a smoother, less-cracked top; if it works we convert the other cheesecakes. Berries/extract are near-zero fat, so macros should stay close to the base - we'll confirm and tune when we talk it through.

payload.instructionText: Make the swirl: simmer the mixed berries with the Splenda and cornstarch over medium heat, mashing, until jammy and thick (4-6 min). Off heat, stir in the imitation blueberry extract. Cool fully before swirling - hot jam melts streaks into the batter.
Once fully chilled, top with a scatter of fresh or thawed mixed berries just before serving.

payload.text: Foil-wrap the pan bottom so the bath can't seep in. Water bath + 300F = slow, even set with no surface overcook, which is what prevents the dome-and-crack. This is the recipe we're using to decide whether to convert all the cheesecakes.

The imitation blueberry extract is potent - 0.5 tsp is plenty; too much reads artificial. The cornstarch keeps the swirl from weeping into the custard. Toss the topping berries in a pinch of cornstarch if they're wet.

payload.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.

Pour about two-thirds of the batter into the parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Spoon dollops of the cooled berry swirl over it, add the rest of the batter, dollop a little more swirl on top, and drag a knife through in S-curves to marble - 3-4 passes only (over-swirling greys the batter). Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake in a WATER BATH at 300F (TEST): wrap the pan bottom in foil, set it inside a larger roasting pan, and pour boiling water halfway up the sides. Bake ~55-70 min until the edges are set and the center still jiggles slightly. The gentle, humid, low heat is the classic anti-crack method - if this finishes smoother and less cracked than our dry 325F bake, we roll the water bath out to the other cheesecakes.
